subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Step 0 - Player-Count Audit
- **Supported counts** — 2, 3, 4, and 5 players.
- **Agent pool table found** — 2 players = 4 Agents each; 3 players = 3 Agents each; 4 players = 2 Agents each; 5 players = 2 Agents each.
- **Round 5 rule found** — Each player places 1 extra Agent near Round 5; it becomes available in Round 5.
- **No solo mode found** — The provided rule text includes no solo/solitaire mode.
- **No other count-scaling tables found** — No additional setup charts with different component counts by player number in provided text.

#### Core box items
- **Board** — 1 game board.
- **Rulebook** — 1 rulebook.
- **Storage tray** — 1 tray.
- **Player mats** — 5 mats.

#### Wooden pieces
- **Total wooden pieces** — 33.
- **Score markers** — 5.
- **Agents** — 25 total (5 of each color).
- **Neutral Agents** — 1 Ambassador and 1 Lieutenant.
- **First player marker** — 1 marker.

#### Adventurer cubes
- **Total cubes** — 100.
- **Clerics** — 25 white cubes.
- **Fighters** — 25 orange cubes.
- **Rogues** — 25 black cubes.
- **Wizards** — 25 purple cubes.

#### Cards
- **Total cards** — 121.
- **Lord cards** — 11.
- **Intrigue cards** — 50.
- **Quest cards** — 60.

#### Die-cut pieces
- **Total die-cut pieces** — 170.
- **Building tiles** — 24.
- **Building control markers** — 45 (9 of each color).
- **Gold tokens** — 60 total (50x1 Gold, 10x5 Gold).
- **VP tokens** — 36 VP tokens plus 5x100 VP tokens.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Board and shared supplies
1. **Center board** — Unfold and place the game board in the middle of the table.
2. **Set supplies** — Place Adventurer cubes, Gold tokens, and VP tokens within easy reach.
3. **Mark rounds** — Place 3 VP tokens on each space of the rounds track.

#### Building setup
4. **Building market** — Draw 3 Building tiles and place them face up in Builder's Hall.
5. **Building stack** — Place remaining Building tiles face down in the labeled stack space.

#### Quest setup
6. **Deal active Quests** — Give each player 2 face-up Quest cards as active Quests.
7. **Inn row** — Place 1 face-up Quest card in each of Cliffwatch Inn's 4 spaces.
8. **Quest deck** — Place remaining Quest cards face down in the labeled Quest deck space.

#### Intrigue setup
9. **Deal Intrigue hands** — Give each player 2 face-down Intrigue cards.
10. **Intrigue deck** — Place remaining Intrigue cards face down near Waterdeep Harbor.

#### Lord setup
11. **Secret Lords** — Shuffle Lord cards and deal 1 face down to each player.
12. **Unused Lords** — Return undealt Lord cards to the box face down.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Per-player items
- **Color choice** — Each player chooses one color.
- **Player mat** — Take the matching mat.
- **Score marker** — Place matching score marker on 0.
- **Control markers** — Take matching Building control markers.

#### Agent pools by player count
--- 2 Players ---
- **Starting Agent pool** — 4 Agents per player in Agent Pool.
- **Round 5 reserve** — 1 additional Agent per player placed near Round 5.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Starting Agent pool** — 3 Agents per player in Agent Pool.
- **Round 5 reserve** — 1 additional Agent per player placed near Round 5.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Starting Agent pool** — 2 Agents per player in Agent Pool.
- **Round 5 reserve** — 1 additional Agent per player placed near Round 5.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Starting Agent pool** — 2 Agents per player in Agent Pool.
- **Round 5 reserve** — 1 additional Agent per player placed near Round 5.

#### Starting cards and hidden info
- **Lord card** — Keep your Lord card face down and secret.
- **Intrigue hand** — Keep your 2 Intrigue cards hidden.
- **Active Quests** — Keep your 2 face-up Quests on your mat.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First player and marker
- **First player rule** — The player who most recently visited another city goes first.
- **Marker** — Give that player the First Player marker.

#### Starting Gold (seat order)
- **First player** — 4 Gold.
- **Next clockwise** — 5 Gold.
- **Next clockwise** — 6 Gold.
- **Continue pattern** — Each next player gets 1 more Gold than the player on their right.

#### Game start state
- **Round length** — Game lasts 8 rounds.
- **Win condition preview** — Most VP after Round 8 wins.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round flow (in order)
1. **Start of round VP movement** — Remove the 3 VP tokens from the current round space.
2. **Builder's Hall growth** — Place 1 VP token on each face-up Building in Builder's Hall.
3. **Start-of-round Building effects** — Resolve any Building text that says "When Purchased/Start of Round."
4. **Player turns** — In turn order, players with available Agents take turns.
5. **Turn options** — On your turn, you may Assign Agent and may Complete Quest.
6. **Skip exhausted players** — Players with no available Agents are skipped for assignment for the rest of the round.
7. **End of round** — Resolve end-of-round return/effect rules, then advance to the next round.
8. **Round 5 unlock** — Each player gains access to their reserved extra Agent in Round 5.
9. **Game end check** — After Round 8, proceed to final scoring.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Assign Agent
- **Core requirement** — If you have available Agents, assign 1 Agent on your turn.
- **Placement target** — Place on any unoccupied action space of a Building in play.
- **Blocked spaces** — You cannot place on occupied spaces.
- **Not-in-play restriction** — You cannot place on Buildings not yet in play.
- **Resolution timing** — Resolve that action space exactly once immediately.

#### Complete Quest
- **Cost payment** — Pay listed Adventurers (and any listed Gold) from your Tavern.
- **Card movement** — Move the completed Quest to Completed Quests area.
- **Reward timing** — Gain Quest reward immediately.

#### Builder's Hall purchase
- **How to buy** — Assigning an Agent to Builder's Hall lets you buy 1 face-up Building.
- **Cost** — Pay the Building's Gold cost.
- **Immediate VP** — Score VP printed for purchasing that Building.
- **Placement** — Put the Building tile into an open board spot.
- **Ownership mark** — Place your control marker on that Building.
- **Refill** — Reveal top Building tile to refill the empty Builder's Hall slot immediately.

#### Waterdeep Harbor
- **Spaces** — Harbor has 3 action spaces.
- **Multi-agent over round** — More than one Agent can be assigned there over the round if spaces remain.
- **Intrigue requirement** — Assigning to Harbor includes playing 1 Intrigue card.
- **No-card restriction** — If you have no Intrigue cards, you cannot assign to Harbor.

#### Card/deck and supply edge cases
- **Limited supply** — If Adventurers of a type run out, hire only what is available.
- **Deck refresh** — If a deck is empty when a draw is required, shuffle its discard to form a new deck.
- **Quest deck exception** — Completed Quests never return to the Quest deck.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Secret Lord scoring
- **Hidden identity** — Your Lord card remains secret during play.
- **Bonus VP source** — Your Lord grants endgame bonus VP for specific conditions.

#### Building ownership economy
- **Owner benefit trigger** — When another player assigns to your Building, you gain its owner benefit.
- **Engine effect** — Purchased Buildings can produce recurring value over multiple rounds.

#### VP token accrual in Builder's Hall
- **Round growth** — Face-up unpurchased Buildings gain VP tokens at round start.
- **Timing pressure** — Delayed purchase can become more valuable if VP accumulates.

#### Neutral Agents
- **Neutral pieces** — Ambassador and Lieutenant start uncontrolled.
- **Gain method** — Specific effects can add them to a player's Agent pool.

#### Mandatory Quest pressure
- **Intrigue interaction** — Some Intrigue effects can force Mandatory Quests.
- **Endgame risk** — Incomplete Mandatory Quests cause VP penalties at final scoring.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End trigger
- **Game end** — End the game after Round 8 is completed.

#### Final scoring order
1. **Track VP** — Keep all VP already earned on the scoring track.
2. **Reveal Lords** — Reveal all Lord cards and score each Lord's bonus VP.
3. **Mandatory penalties** — Subtract 4 VP for each incomplete Mandatory Quest.
4. **Endgame effects** — Apply any additional endgame VP from card/Building text.

#### VP sources checklist
- **Quest VP** — VP from completed Quests.
- **Building VP** — VP from purchasing Buildings and VP-granting Building effects.
- **Intrigue VP** — VP from Intrigue effects that award points.
- **Lord bonus VP** — VP from hidden Lord objectives after reveal.
- **Penalty VP** — -4 VP per incomplete Mandatory Quest.

#### Winner and tie-break
- **Winner** — Highest total VP wins.
- **First tie-break** — If tied, highest leftover resources wins (Gold + Adventurer cubes in Tavern).
- **Second tie-break** — If still tied, tied players share victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early-round priorities (Rounds 1-2)
- **Resource engine first** — Take actions that reliably produce Adventurers and Gold before chasing marginal VP.
- **Quest overlap** — Draft Quests that reuse the same Adventurer colors to reduce dead resources.
- **Harbor readiness** — Keep at least one Intrigue option live so Harbor placements are always meaningful.
- **Builder's Hall timing** — Track Buildings with both strong action text and accumulating VP tokens.
- **Round 5 prep** — Enter Round 5 with at least one high-value line ready for your extra Agent turn.

#### Hidden-information discipline
- **Lord camouflage** — Avoid telegraphing your Lord objective with overly narrow early Quest picks.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Cube hoarding** — Stockpiling Adventurers without converting to Quests delays scoring tempo.
- **Low-impact Building buys** — Paying Gold for weak owner benefits often loses long-term efficiency.
- **Harbor neglect** — Ignoring Intrigue timing gives up major disruption and tempo plays.
- **Identity leakage** — Repeating one Quest category too early reveals your Lord and invites denial.
- **Mandatory Quest neglect** — Leaving Mandatory Quests unfinished can cost 4 VP each at endgame.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: VP now vs engine later
- **Immediate line** — Complete a Quest now for guaranteed points and board position.
- **Engine line** — Buy/build toward recurring income or owner benefits for later rounds.

#### Decision 2: Strong move vs hidden agenda
- **Visible efficiency** — Best raw-value move may expose your Lord objective.
- **Concealment line** — Slightly weaker move can preserve secrecy and reduce targeted disruption.

#### Decision 3: Advance self vs block opponent
- **Self-optimization** — Take the best action for your own plan.
- **Denial play** — Occupy a contested space to break an opponent's high-value turn, especially in Rounds 6-8.

metadata.notes: Reformatted for scan-first readability with explicit player-count audit and count-specific headers where rules vary.
